# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

const knownModules: Record<string, string> = {
  "moduleA": "https://deno.land/x/module_a/mod.ts", // Example of a known module
  // You could map short names to full URLs here
};

async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);

  // 1. Get module path from query parameters
  let modulePath = url.searchParams.get("module");

  // 2. Fallback to environment variable if not in query
  if (!modulePath) {
    modulePath = Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");
  }

  // 3. Fallback to a known module if a short name is used
  if (modulePath && knownModules[modulePath]) {
    modulePath = knownModules[modulePath];
  }

  if (!modulePath) {
    return new Response("Error: No module path provided. Use ?module=<url_or_name> or set MODULE_PATH environment variable.", { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    // Dynamically import the module
    const module = await import(modulePath);

    // Assuming the module exports a default function or an 'execute' function
    let result: unknown;
    if (typeof module.default === "function") {
      result = await module.default(req, url); // Pass request and URL for context
    } else if (typeof module.execute === "function") {
      result = await module.execute(req, url); // Another common pattern
    } else {
      return new Response(`Error: Module ${modulePath} does not export a default function or an 'execute' function.`, { status: 500 });
    }

    // Return the result as JSON or plain text
    if (typeof result === "object" && result !== null) {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    } else {
      return new Response(String(result), {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "text/plain" },
      });
    }

  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`Failed to load or execute module ${modulePath}:`, error);
    return new Response(`Error loading or executing module ${modulePath}: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
}

serve(handler);
